questions.question: Define a rooted tree and explain the concepts of depth and height of a node in a rooted tree. How are depth and height related for any node in the tree?
keyPoints: Rooted tree: A tree with a distinguished root node where every edge is directed away from root
Depth of a node: Number of edges from root to that node (root has depth 0)
Height of a node: Number of edges on longest path from that node to a leaf
Height of tree = height of root; Height = max(depth) + 1 for perfect tree

questions.question: Define the following path length concepts in trees: (i) Internal path length (ii) External path length (iii) Weighted path length with an example of each.
keyPoints: Internal path length: Sum of depths of all internal nodes in the tree
External path length: Sum of depths of all external (null) nodes or leaf nodes
Weighted path length: Sum of (weight × depth) for all nodes; used in optimal binary search trees
External path length = Internal path length + 2n for full binary tree (n internal nodes)

questions.question: Define a binary tree and explain the properties of binary trees. Also explain the relationship between number of nodes and edges in any tree, and prove that a binary tree with n nodes has exactly n-1 edges.
keyPoints: Binary tree: Rooted tree where each node has at most two children (left and right)
Properties: Maximum nodes at level i = 2^(i-1), Maximum nodes in tree of height h = 2^h - 1
In any tree (acyclic graph), edges = vertices - 1 (proved by induction or removing leaves)
Full binary tree: Every node has 0 or 2 children; Complete binary tree: All levels filled except last

questions.question: Explain the differences between binary trees, binary search trees (BST), and AVL trees. Also discuss the concept of balance factor in AVL trees and how rotation operations maintain balance with an example.
keyPoints: Binary tree: General tree with max 2 children; BST: Binary tree with left < root < right property for all nodes
AVL tree: Self-balancing BST where balance factor (height of left - height of right) is -1, 0, or 1 for all nodes
Rotation operations: LL (right rotation), RR (left rotation), LR (left then right), RL (right then left) rotations
AVL rotations restore balance factor while maintaining BST property; height of AVL tree is O(log n)
